tagline: Inspect, read, and safely plan typed Gong operations.

global_flags.name: credential
global_flags.summary: Credential name to use for the Gong request.

global_flags.name: limit
global_flags.summary: Maximum ETL records to emit for stream commands.

global_flags.name: max-bytes
global_flags.summary: Maximum direct-read response bytes, capped at 1 MiB.

global_flags.name: plan
global_flags.summary: Execute an approved reverse-ETL plan by id.

global_flags.name: preview
global_flags.summary: Preview a reverse-ETL write command without making a network mutation.

global_flags.name: approve
global_flags.summary: Approve a reverse-ETL plan before execution.

global_flags.name: confirm
global_flags.summary: Typed confirmation challenge for destructive reverse-ETL writes.

commands.summary: sends call, engagement, or digital interaction content to Gong; requires reverse ETL approval

flags.name: actualStart
flags.summary: The actual date and time when the call started in the ISO-8601 format (e.g., '2018-02-18T02:30:00-07:00' or '2018-02-18T08:00:00Z', where Z stands for UTC);

flags.name: clientUniqueId
flags.summary: A call's unique identifier in the PBX or the recording system. Gong uses this identifier to prevent repeated attempts to upload the same recording.

flags.name: direction
flags.summary: Whether the call is inbound (someone called the company), outbound (a rep dialed someone outside the company), or a conference call.

flags.name: parties
flags.summary: A list of the call's participants. A party must be provided for the primaryUser.

flags.name: primaryUser
flags.summary: The Gong internal user ID of the team member who hosted the call.

commands.risk: high: sends call, engagement, or digital interaction content to Gong; requires reverse ETL approval
commands.approval: Use reverse ETL plan -> preview -> approval -> execute. Connector command execution is metadata-only for complex object/array records; use typed reverse ETL records.
commands.notes: No raw HTTP body is accepted. Object and array payloads must come from typed reverse-ETL records validated by writes.json.

help_topics.name: gong-auth
help_topics.summary: Use Gong access key and access key secret via credentials; never pass secrets in command text.

help_topics.name: gong-writes
help_topics.summary: Gong mutations are typed reverse-ETL actions with plan, preview, approval, execute gates.

help_topics.name: gong-direct-read
help_topics.summary: Gong direct reads are bounded JSON GET operations with redaction for secret/download/content-shaped fields.
